title: Treasury and IRS to Provide Guidance to Religious Organizations
summary: The U.S. Department of the Treasury and IRS announced plans to develop guidance on the Johnson Amendment, clarifying how tax rules apply to communications by houses of worship within religious services. The announcement responds to litigation (National Religious Broadcasters v. Bessent) and aims to provide administrable standards for religious organizations regarding political campaign intervention prohibitions. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent framed this as protecting religious freedom ahead of the nation's 250th anniversary.
key_points: Treasury and IRS will issue guidance clarifying the Johnson Amendment's application to houses of worship
The guidance will address bona fide internal communications within religious contexts and communications between houses of worship and their congregations
The initiative responds to National Religious Broadcasters v. Bessent, where a federal district court dismissed the case before approving a proposed resolution
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent framed this as religious liberty protection ahead of the nation's 250th anniversary year
Stakeholder engagement is planned during development; release timing is undetermined
why_it_matters: This is a tax policy announcement about religious organizations and political activity restrictions under the Johnson Amendment. It has no relevance to financial markets, monetary policy, interest rates, economic data, or market structure.
